comments.text: This problem is "claimed" to have been resolved by E. Li in [Li26].

The end reads OpenAI’s ChatGPT was used for ideation, formulation, proof exploration and refinement, narrowing the search space, programming, LATEX formatting and other forms of orchestration, the author nonetheless takes full responsibility for the accuracy of the final contents of this paper. So another affirmative paper almost purely by AI.

If the proof is correct, yes, but like the rest of Eric's recent papers I find this AI-style of writing proofs to be difficult to read, and I am reserving judgement on whether any of these proofs are correct until I am confident that humans have read and vouched for the proof.
GPT-5.5 Thinking identifies some issues here, but the fatal ones appear fixable.

I checked the alleged “fatal issues" carefully, and they are not present in the proof. They appear to come from dropped overlines in automated PDF/math extraction rather than from the actual displayed statements in the paper. Even when the PDF file itself is uploaded, the parsing layer can still lose accents such as $\overline{G_{\mathrm b}}$. In the future, I would strongly recommend checking the displayed PDF visually, or preferably the TeX source from arXiv, for these statements. I of course welcome careful scrutiny of the proof, but these particular objections are not issues in the paper as written.
Yes, I see. It appears GPT didn’t parse the PDF correctly. I've sent you everything GPT found using the TeX file, I also briefly checked that it parsed everything properly.

I have run a screening check on this paper; no issue is found. Note that for a non-short paper, the effectiveness decreases accordingly. Literature-wise, there are some recommended citations. See the third-to-last message. Of particular interest is that the Ajtai–Komlós–Simonovits–Szemerédi proof of the Erdős–Sós conjecture (which appears to be unpublished?) subsumes some part of the paper. Note I'm very much not an expert here so this is just more data and doesn't vouch for the paper in any way.
